channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's criticism of NATO raises questions of whether — or how — the U.S. could leave
item.description: President Trump has complained that some NATO states aren't letting him use bases in the war against Iran. His criticism has left many wondering if the U.S. may try to withdraw from the alliance.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has complained that some NATO states aren't letting him use bases in the war against Iran. His criticism has left many wondering if the U.S. may try to withdraw from the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to get internet
item.description: As the Israel and the U.S. continue striking Iran, Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey to find an Internet connection with access cut off in Iran
item.content:encoded: As the Israel and the U.S. continue striking Iran, Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey to find an Internet connection with access cut off in Iran
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Seville, Spain's Holy Week blends faith, tradition and spectacle
item.description: Even as religious belief declines in Spain, the processions at Seville's Semana Santa — the Holy Week lead-up to Easter — draw crowds moved by music, tradition and powerful emotion.
item.content:encoded:
Even as religious belief declines in Spain, the processions at Seville's Semana Santa — the Holy Week lead-up to Easter — draw crowds moved by music, tradition and powerful emotion.

(Image credit: Fran Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Alexis Marshall

item.title: A U.S. jet goes down over Iran, a U.S. official confirms
item.description: A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: China's Communist Party investigates ex-Xinjiang leader Ma Xingrui
item.description: Ma Xingrui is a member of the party's Central Committee and served as party secretary of the Xinjiang region in China's northwest from 2021-2025.
item.content:encoded: Ma Xingrui is a member of the party's Central Committee and served as party secretary of the Xinjiang region in China's northwest from 2021-2025.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pam Bondi is out at DOJ. And, NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit
item.description: President Trump announced yesterday that Pam Bondi is out as Attorney General. And, NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit and is heading toward the moon.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced yesterday that Pam Bondi is out as Attorney General. And, NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit and is heading toward the moon.

(Image credit: Matt McClain)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 2 U.S. planes are down and Iran hits Gulf refineries as the war wraps its 5th week
item.description: An F-15 went down in Iran and a second Air Force plane crashed near the Strait of Hormuz as the war capped a week of intensified fighting.
item.content:encoded:
An F-15 went down in Iran and a second Air Force plane crashed near the Strait of Hormuz as the war capped a week of intensified fighting.

(Image credit: Morteza Nikoubazl)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Evacuation of U.S. troops from Mideast base sends community groups scrambling to help
item.description: Troops and their families have been pushed back to the United States after their bases in the Middle East were threatened by Iranian counterattacks. Community groups are scrambling to react.
item.content:encoded:
Troops and their families have been pushed back to the United States after their bases in the Middle East were threatened by Iranian counterattacks. Community groups are scrambling to react.

(Image credit: Fadhel Madhan)
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Former U.S. ambassador talks about Trump's rhetoric on NATO
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks Dan Baer, former U.S. ambassador to the Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe, about the Trump administration's recent rhetoric around NATO.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks Dan Baer, former U.S. ambassador to the Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe, about the Trump administration's recent rhetoric around NATO.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closure deflates global helium supply
item.description: The blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is affecting not only oil, but the global helium supply.
item.content:encoded: The blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is affecting not only oil, but the global helium supply.
item.dc:creator: Lilly Quiroz
